question: How would you describe your life after graduation?
answer: After I graduate, I plan to find a stable job in IT, maybe in Ho Chi Minh City, abroad, or as a freelancer. My real dream is to learn music production someday because music is my passion. My mom always told me to get a career first, then follow my dreams. So that is my plan, and I will try my best.

question: How long have you practiced speaking English?
answer: I have been studying English since primary school, so it has been more than 10 years. Honestly, my English is still not very strong. I only started practicing speaking seriously at university. I still feel nervous sometimes, but I try my best to improve every day.

question: Can you describe your classroom on Monday?
answer: On Monday, I have an online English class with around 40 to 50 students. We interact through apps and websites, and many students share their answers online. It feels like a normal classroom but through a screen. The atmosphere is quite academic but also relaxed.

question: Is it important to make a lot of money?
answer: I think money is important because if I have enough money, I can travel and explore new places with my friends or family. Money also reduces stress in daily life. But health and happiness matter too, so I want a job that pays well and that I actually enjoy doing.

question: What tasks are involved in your day at the university?
answer: My typical day includes attending lectures, taking notes, and doing group projects. I also spend time learning new technology tools related to my major. After class, I usually do assignments or review my lessons. It keeps me quite busy, but I enjoy learning new things in my field.

question: How does technology help us in our everyday lives?
answer: Technology helps us communicate, search for information, and pay for things easily. As an IT student, I use my laptop every day for studying and projects. What I find most exciting is AI because it helps me a lot with my coursework. A task that used to take several people days can now be finished much faster.

question: Which invention brings more benefits, a washing machine or a laptop?
answer: Definitely a laptop, especially for a student like me. I use it every day for studying, research, and entertainment. A washing machine is helpful, but it only does one main task. A laptop can do hundreds of tasks, so for me the laptop is more useful.

question: Are you interested in ancient languages?
answer: To be honest, I am not very familiar with them. But I think ancient languages are interesting because they connect us to history and culture. If I had more free time, I would love to explore Latin or ancient Greek. For now, I am focused on improving my English first.

question: What will happen if you are excellent at two foreign languages?
answer: I think my career opportunities would increase a lot. I could work for international companies or even abroad. Personally, the most exciting part is travel and exploration. If I knew another popular language, I could talk to different people and change my mindset.

question: Tell me about your last holiday.
answer: My last holiday was Tet, a few months ago. I went home, visited relatives, ate traditional food, and watched fireworks with my family. More than just fun, Tet felt like a time to heal because I had been running deadlines and sleeping very little before that. During the break, I reflected on my life and made plans for the future.

question: What had you done before going to bed last night?
answer: Before going to bed, I finished my homework, had dinner, took a shower, and chatted with some friends. I also spent a little time with my family, which I really value. Being with my parents, grandmother, and sister at home means a lot to me. On difficult days in the future, I think I will remember those quiet evenings and feel motivated.

question: Do you think it is a fascinating experience when driving along the coast?
answer: Honestly, I have never tried it before, so I am not sure. But I think it sounds like a wonderful experience because you can see the ocean, feel the fresh air, and enjoy beautiful views. I would love to try it someday, maybe with my friends or family.

question: How would you spend your upcoming holiday?
answer: I am planning a trip to Da Lat with my high school friends. We have already made plans and we are saving money for it. I am really looking forward to the night market, grilled rice paper, hot soy milk, avocado ice cream, and the cool misty weather. It will be refreshing after the stress of studying.

question: Is it free to go to a museum in your country?
answer: In Vietnam, most museums have a small entrance fee, but it is usually very affordable. I once visited the War Remnants Museum in Ho Chi Minh City, and it was very meaningful. There were tanks, anti-aircraft guns, helicopters, and many historical artifacts. I think more people should visit places like that.

question: What are the disadvantages of living in the countryside?
answer: There are fewer job opportunities in the countryside, especially in technology. Hospitals and schools are also more limited. My hometown is near the Mekong River, so during the rainy season the water rises very high. It can flood homes and make farming difficult, so many young people move to Ho Chi Minh City.

question: How far can technology advance in the next 50 years?
answer: I think technology can advance very far in the next 50 years. AI, robots, and smart devices may become a normal part of daily life, and they could help people study, work, and take care of their health. As an IT student, I feel excited about this future, but I also think we need to use technology carefully and responsibly.
